mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding aggregate income and consumption, which drives corporate revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for month-over-month PAYEMS changes falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate greater than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals labor market deterioration that precedes earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE has risen >0.3pp over the prior 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in consumer prices and the federal funds rate create a restrictive real financial conditions shock that reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL changes and FEDFUNDS changes generates a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During high interest-rate regimes, month-over-month declines in real retail sales signal acute demand destruction that forces margin compression and inventory write-downs, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, month-over-month RSAFS declines are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month contraction in industrial production presages inventory liquidation and declining capacity utilization, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative month-over-month INDPRO changes are associated with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample observations.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained levels of new housing starts above 1,500 thousand units reflect durable demand strength and construction sector optimism that spills over into broad equity performance, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1500, subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns are negative more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Joint expansion in real GDP and payrolls confirms a broad-based demand recovery that reduces recession probability and lifts equity risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term between GDPC1 and PAYEMS changes is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 2.0% signals highly accommodative short-term financing conditions that inflate near-term equity risk premiums through carry-trade and leverage dynamics, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods with FEDFUNDS < 2.0%, 5-day forward returns are negative more than 50% of the time.
